subject: MEASURE
entry:
-(The following modern equivalents of ancient measurements are based upon the latest research, and are as correct as is possible at this time)
-DRY
-1. A bushel, about a peck MAT 5:15; MRK 4:21; LUK 11:33
-2. A cab, or kab, about two quarts 2KI 6:25
-3. A cor, equal to one homer or ten ephahs, equal to about eleven and one-ninth bushels 1KI 4:22; 5:11; 2CH 2:10; 27:5; EZR 7:22
-4. An ephah, equal to three seah, and in liquid, to a bath, containing about a bushel and a half EXO 16:36; LEV 5:11; 6:20; 19:36; NUM 5:15; 28:5; JDG 6:19; RUT 2:17; 1SA 1:24; 17:17; ISA 5:10; EZK 45:10,11,13,24; 46:5,7,11,14; AMO 8:5; ZEC 5:6-10
-5. A half-homer, about five and a half bushels HOS 3:2
-6. A homer, about eleven bushels, equal to a cor or ten ephahs LEV 27:16; NUM 11:32; ISA 5:10; EZK 45:11-14; HOS 3:2
-7. An omer, about one bushel EXO 16:16,18
-8. A seah, about a peck and a half GEN 18:6; 1SA 25:18; 1KI 18:32; 2KI 7:1,16,18

-1. Satu gantang, kira-kira satu kecupan MAT 5:15; Mrk 4:21; Lukas 11:33

-6. Satu homer, kira-kira sebelas gantang, sama dengan satu kor atau sepuluh efah LEV 27:16; Bilangan 11:32; ISA 5:10; EZK 45:11-14; HOS 3:2
-7. Satu omer, kira-kira satu gantang EXO 16:16,18
-8. Satu seah, kira-kira satu setengah kecupan KEJADIAN 18:6; 1Sam 25:18; 1KI 18:32; 2KI 7:1,16,18

-2. Satu pohon cemara, hampir sembilan galon JHN 2:6
-3. Satu hin, sekitar satu setengah galon EXO 29:40; 30:24; IM 19:36; 23:13; Bilangan 15:4-10; 28:5,7,14; EZK 4:11
-4. Satu batang kayu, kira-kira satu pint, seperdua belas hin LEV 14:10,12,15,21,24

-1. Sebuah jari JER 52:21
-2. selebar tangan EXO 25:25; 37:12; 1KI 7:26; 2CH 4:5; PSA 39:5; EZK 40:5,43; 43:13
-3. Rentang EXO 28:16; 1Sam 17:4; ISA 40:12; 48:13; LAM 2:20; EZK 43:13

     -Stars in their courses fought against Sisera JDG 5:20
     -Stones from heaven JOS 10:11
     -Fire from heaven at Elijah's command 2KI 1:10-14
     -The whirlwind which carried Elijah to heaven 2KI 2:1,11
     -The wind under God's control PSA 107:25
     -The east wind PSA 48:7
     -Rain, The formation of PSA 135:6,7
     -Dew, copious PSA 133:3

     -Rain confuses the Philistine army 1SA 7:10

subject: MONTH
entry:
-Ancient use of GEN 7:11; 8:4
-Twelve months reckoned to a year 1CH 27:1-15
-1. Abib (April)
     -The Jewish calendar began with EXO 12:2; 13:4; DEU 16:1
     -Passover instituted and celebrated in EXO 12:1-28; 23:15
     -Israelites left Egypt in EXO 13:4
     -Tabernacle set up in EXO 40:2,17
     -Israelites arrive at Zin in NUM 20:1
     -The Israelites cross the Jordan River in JOS 4:19
     -The Jordan River overflows in 1CH 12:15
     -Decree to put the Jews to death in EST 3:12
     -The death of Jesus in MAT 26; 27
     -After the captivity called Nisan NEH 2:1; EST 3:7
-2. Zif (May) 1KI 6:1,37
     -Israel numbered in NUM 1:1,18
     -Passover to be observed in, by the ceremonially unclean and others who could not observe it in the first month NUM 9:10,11
     -Israel departed from the wilderness of Zin in NUM 10:11
     -Temple begun in 1KI 6:1; 2CH 3:2
     -An celebrated in 2CH 30:1-27
     -Rebuilding of the temple begun in EZR 3:8
-3. Sivan: (June) EST 8:9
     -Asa renews the covenant of himself and people in 2CH 15:10
-4. Tammuz (July)
-(The number only appears in the Bible) .Jerusalem taken by Nebuchadnezzar in JER 39:2; 52:6,7
-5. Ab (August)
-(Number only mentioned)
     -Aaron died on the first day of NUM 33:38
     -Temple destroyed in 2KI 25:8-10; JER 1:3; 52:12-30
     -Ezra arrived at Jerusalem in EZR 7:8,9
-6. Elul (September)
     -The wall of Jerusalem finished in NEH 6:15
     -The temple built in HAG 1:14,15
-7. Ethanim (October) 1KI 8:2
     -Feasts held in LEV 23:24,27; NEH 8:13-15
     -Jubilee proclaimed in LEV 25:9
     -Solomon's temple dedicated in 1KI 8:2
     -Altar rebuilt and offerings renewed in EZR 3:1,6
-8. Bul (November)
     -The temple finished in 1KI 6:38
     -Jeroboam's idolatrous feast in 1KI 12:32,33; 1CH 27:11
-9. Chisleu (December) EZR 10:9; JER 36:9,22; ZEC 7:1
-10. Tebeth (January) EST 2:16
     -Nebuchadnezzar besieges Jerusalem in 2KI 25:1; JER 52:4
-11. Sebat (February) ZEC 1:7
     -Moses probably died in DEU 1:3
-12. Adar (March) EST 3:7
     -Second temple finished in EZR 6:15
     -Feast of Purim in EST 9:1-26
     -Months in prophecy REV 11:2
entry_id:
-Penggunaan kuno dari GEN 7:11; 8:4
-Dua belas bulan diperhitungkan dalam satu tahun 1CH 27:1-15
-1. Abib (April)
     -Kalender Yahudi dimulai dengan EXO 12:2; 13:4; ULAN 16:1
     -Paskah dilembagakan dan dirayakan dalam EXO 12:1-28; 23:15
     -Orang Israel meninggalkan Mesir di EXO 13:4
     -Tabernakel didirikan di EXO 40:2,17
     -Orang Israel tiba di Zin dalam Bilangan 20:1
     -Bangsa Israel menyeberangi Sungai Yordan dalam JOS 4:19
     -Sungai Yordan meluap dalam 1CH 12:15
     -Keputusan untuk membunuh orang Yahudi di EST 3:12
     -Kematian Yesus di MAT 26; 27
     -Setelah pembuangan disebut Nisan NEH 2:1; Est 3:7
-2. Zif (Mei) 1KI 6:1,37
     -Israel bernomor di NUM 1:1,18
     -Paskah yang harus dirayakan oleh orang yang najis dan orang lain yang tidak dapat merayakannya pada bulan pertama NUM 9:10,11
     -Israel berangkat dari padang gurun Zin di Bilangan 10:11
     -Kuil dimulai dalam 1KI 6:1; 2CH 3:2
     -Dirayakan dalam 2CH 30:1-27
     -Pembangunan kembali bait suci dimulai dalam EZR 3:8
-3. Sivan: (Juni) EST 8:9
     -Asa memperbarui perjanjian dirinya dan orang-orang dalam 2CH 15:10
-4. Tamuz (Juli)
-(Angka tersebut hanya muncul dalam Alkitab) .Yerusalem diambil oleh Nebukadnezar dalam YER 39:2; 52:6,7
-5. Ab (Agustus)
-(Nomor hanya disebutkan)
     -Aaron meninggal pada hari pertama Bilangan 33:38
     -Kuil dihancurkan dalam 2KI 25:8-10; Yer 1:3; 52:12-30
     -Ezra tiba di Yerusalem dalam EZR 7:8,9
-6. Elul (September)

subject: MOSES
entry:
-A Levite and son of Amram EXO 2:1-4; 6:20; ACT 7:20; HEB 11:23
-Hidden in a small basket EXO 2:3
-Discovered and adopted by the daughter of Pharaoh EXO 2:5-10
-Learned in all the wisdom of Egypt ACT 7:22
-His loyalty to his race HEB 11:24-26
-Takes the life of an Egyptian taskmaster; flees from Egypt; finds refuge among the Midianites EXO 2:11-22; ACT 7:24-29
-Joins himself to Jethro, priest of Midian; marries his daughter Zipporah; has one son, Gershom EXO 2:15-22
-Is a herdsman for Jethro in the desert of Horeb EXO 3:1
-Has the vision of the burning bush EXO 3:2-6
-God reveals to him his purpose to deliver the Israelites and bring them into the land of Canaan EXO 3:7-10
-Commissioned as leader of the Israelites EXO 3:10-22; 6:13
-His rod miraculously turned into a serpent, and his hand was made leprous, and then restored EXO 4:1-9,28
-With his wife and sons, he leaves Jethro to perform his mission in Egypt EXO 4:18-20
-His controversy with his wife on account of circumcision EXO 4:20-26
-Meets Aaron in the wilderness EXO 4:27,28
-With Aaron assembles the leaders of Israel EXO 4:29-31
-Along with Aaron, Moses goes before Pharaoh and demands the liberties of his people in the name of Jehovah EXO 5:1
-Rejected by Pharaoh; hardships of the Israelites increased EXO 5
-People complain against Moses and Aaron EXO 5:20,21; 15:24; 16:2,3; 17:2,3; NUM 14:2-4; 16:41; 20:2-5; 21:4-6; DEU 1:12,26-28
-See ISRAEL
-Receives comfort and assurance from the Lord EXO 6:1-8
-Unbelief of the people EXO 6:9
-Renews his appeal to Pharaoh EXO 6:11
-Under divine direction brings plagues upon the land of Egypt EXO 7; 8; 9; 10; 11; 12
-Secures the deliverance of the people and leads them out of Egypt EXO 13
-Crosses the Red Sea; Pharaoh and his army are destroyed EXO 14
-Composes a song for the people of Israel on their deliverance from Pharaoh EXO 15
-Joined by his family in the wilderness EXO 18:1-12
-Institutes a system of government EXO 18:13-26; NUM 11:16-30; DEU 1:9-18
-Receives the law and ordains various statutes
-See LAW OF MOSES
-Face of, transfigured EXO 34:29-35; 2CO 3:13
-Sets up the tabernacle -See TABERNACLE
-Reproves Aaron for
     -Making the golden calf EXO 32:22,23
     -Irregularity in the offerings LEV 10:16-20
-Jealousy of Aaron and Miriam toward NUM 12
-Rebellion of Korah, Dathan, and Abiram against NUM 16
-Appoints Joshua as his successor NUM 27:22,23; DEU 31:7,8,14,23; 34:9
-Not permitted to enter Canaan, but views the land from atop Mount Pisgah NUM 27:12-14; DEU 1:37; 3:23-29; 32:48-52; 34:1-8
-Death and burial of NUM 31:2; DEU 32:50; 34:1-6
-Body of, disputed over Jude 1:9
-120 Years old at death DEU 31:2
-Mourning for, thirty days in the plains of Moab DEU 34:8
-His virility DEU 31:2; 34:7
-Present with Jesus on the Mount of Transfiguration MAT 17:3,4; MRK 9:4; LUK 9:30
-A type of the Messiah DEU 18:15-18; ACT 3:22; 7:37
-BENEDICTIONS OF
     -Upon the people LEV 9:23; NUM 10:35,36; DEU 1:11
     -Last benediction upon the twelve tribes DEU 33
-CHARACTER OF
     -Complainings of EXO 5:22,23; NUM 11:10-15
     -Impatience of EXO 5:22,23; 6:12; 32:19; NUM 11:10-15; 16:15; 20:10; 31:14
     -Respected and feared EXO 33:8
     -Faith of NUM 10:29; DEU 9:1-3; HEB 11:23-28
     -Called the man of God DEU 33:1

     -Magnanimity of, toward Eldad and Medad NUM 11:29
     -Meekness of EXO 14:13,14; 15:24,25; 16:2,3,7,8; NUM 12:3; 16:4-11

subject: MUSIC
entry:
-Teachers of 1CH 15:22; 25:7,8; 2CH 23:13
-Physical effect of, on man 1SA 6:15,16
-Discoursed during the offering of sacrifices 2CH 29:27,28
-Precentor NEH 12:42
-Chief musician NEH 12:42; HAB 3:19
-Chambers for musicians in the temple, in Ezekiel's vision EZK 40:44
-In heaven REV 5:8,9; 14:2,3; 15:2,3
-INSTRUMENTS OF
     -Invented by Jubal GEN 4:21
     -Invented by David 1CH 23:5; 2CH 7:6; 29:26; AMO 6:5
     -Made by Solomon 1KI 10:12; 2CH 9:11; ECC 2:8
     -Made by Tyrians EZK 28:13
     -CORNET DAN 3:5,7,10
     -See TRUMPET
     -CYMBAL
     -See CYMBAL
     -DULCIMER, a double pipe DAN 3:5,10,15
     -FLUTE DAN 3:5,7,10,15
     -GITTITH, a stringed instrument PSA 8; 81; 84
     -HARP
     -See HARP
     -ORGAN, probably composed of pipes furnishing a number of notes GEN 4:21; JOB 21:12; 30:31; PSA 150:4
     -PIPE
     -See PIPE
     -PSALTERY
     -See PSALTERY
     -SACKBUT, a harp DAN 3:5,7,10,15
     -TABRET
     -See TIMBREL
     -TIMBREL, a tambourine
     -See TIMBREL
     -TRUMPET
     -See TRUMPET
     -VIOL, a lyre ISA 5:12; 14:11; AMO 5:23; 6:5
-SYMBOLS USED IN
-ALAMOTH
     -(Literally virgins) .A musical term which appears in 1CH 15:20
     -And in the title of PSA 46:1
     -(It seems to indicate the rendering of the song by female voices, possibly soprano) -AL-TASCHITH
     -It appears in the titles of PSA 57:1; 58:1; 59:1; 75:1
     -(it seems to have been used to indicate the kind of ode, or the kind of melody in which the ode should be sung.)
-HIGGAION PSA 92:3
     -(According to Gesenius, it signifies the murmuring tone of a harp, and hence that the music should be rendered in a plaintive manner.) PSA 9:16
     -(Combined with "Selah," it may have been intended to indicate a pause in the vocal music while the instruments rendered an interlude.) PSA 19:14
     -(Mendelssohn translates it "meditation, thought." Hence, the music was to be rendered in a mode to promote devout meditation.)
-MAKALATH, MASCHIL, LEANNOTH
     -These terms are found in the titles of PSA 53:1; 88:1
     -(Authorities grope in darkness as to their signification. They may indicate the instruments to be played, or the melody to be sung)
-MASCHIL
     -This musical sign occurs in the titles of PSA 32:1; 42:1; 44:1; 45:1; 52:1; 53:1; 54:1; 55:1; 74:1; 78:1; 88:1; 89:1; 142:1
     -(The meaning is obscure. But its signification where it occurs elsewhere than in the titles of Psalms is equivalent to the English word "instruction," or to become wise by instruction; hence, PSA 47:7: "Sing ye praises with understanding.")
-MICAHTAM
     -A musical term in the titles of PSA 16:1; 56:1; 57:1; 58:1; 59:1; 60:1
     -(Luther interprets this as "golden," that is, "precious." Ewald interprets it as signifying a plaintive manner.)
-MUTH-LABBEN
     -In the title of PSA 9:1
     -(Authorities, ancient and modern, differ as to the probable signification. Gesenius and DEU Wette interpret it, "with the voice of virgins, by boys." Others derive the word from a different Hebrew root, and interpret it as indicating that the Psalm was a funeral ode.)
-NEGINAH and NEGINOTH
     -Appear in the titles of PSA 4:1; 54:1; 55:1; 61:1; 67:1; HAB 3:19
     -(Its use seems to have been to indicate that the song should be accompanied by stringed instruments.) -NEHILOTH
     -Appears in the title of PSA 5:1
     -(It seems to indicate, according to Gesenius, that when this Psalm was sung it was to be accompanied by wind instruments.)
-SELAH
     -(This term appears frequently in the Psalms. Its use is not known. Possibly it signified a pause in the vocal music while an instrumental interlude or finale was rendered.)
-SHEMINITH
     -In the titles of PSA 6:1; 12:1
     -(Translated "eighth," probably indicates the measure, movement, or pitch.)
-SHIGGAION
     -In the title of PSA 7:1
     -And its plural, SHIGIONOTH, in the title of HAB 3:1
     -(Supposed to be a musical term to guide in rendering the song. At the close of the chapter, the author refers the ode "to the chief musician, on my stringed instruments." The term may suggest the movement in interpreting the music set to it.)
-SHOSHANNIM and SHUSHAN-EDUTH .In the titles to PSA 45:1; 60:1; 69:1; 80:1
     -(Seems to indicate the manner in which these Psalms were to be rendered. Kimchi, Tremellius, and Eichhorn render it "hexachorda," that is, that in singing these Psalms instruments of six strings were to accompany.)
-UNCLASSIFIED SCRIPTURES RELATING TO GEN 31:27; EXO 15:1,20,21; 21:17-20; JDG 5:1-3; 11:34; 1SA 18:6,7; 2SA 19:35; 1KI 1:40; 1CH 6:31,32; 15:16-22,24,27,28; 16:4-39,41,42; 22:3,5,6,27,30; 25:1,5,6,7,8; 2CH 5:12,13; 20:19,21,22,28; 23:13,18; 29:25-28; 31:2; 35:15,25; EZR 2:64; 3:10,11; NEH 12:24,27-31; JOB 21:12; 30:31; PSA 33:1-4; 68:4,25,26,32-35; 81:1-3; 87:7; 92:1-3; 95:1,2; 98:1-8; 104:33; 105:2; 135:1,2,3; 137:1-6; 144:9; 149:1-3,6; 150:1-6; PRO 25:20; ECC 2:8; ISA 5:12; 14:11; 16:10; 23:16; 24:8,9; 30:29,32; JER 31:4; EZK 26:13; 33:32; DAN 3:4,6,7; AMO 6:5; 8:10; MAT 9:23; 11:17; MRK 14:26; 1CO 14:15; EPH 5:19; COL 3:16; HEB 2:12; REV 14:2,3; 18:22
-See PRAISE
-See PSALMS

subject: NOAH
entry:
-1. Son of Lamech GEN 5:28,29
     -Builds an ark (ship) and saves his family from the great flood GEN 6:14-22; 7; 8; MAT 24:38; LUK 17:27; HEB 11:7; 1PE 3:20
     -Builds an altar and offers sacrifices GEN 8:20,21
     -Receives the covenant from God that no flood would ever again visit the earth; the rainbow instituted as a token of the covenant GEN 8:20,22; 9:9-17
     -Intoxication of, and his curse upon Canaan GEN 9:20-27
     -His blessing upon Shem and Japheth GEN 9:26,27
     -Dies at the age of nine-hundred and fifty years GEN 9:28,29
-2. A daughter of Zelophehad, special legislation in regard to the inheritance of NUM 26:33; 27:1-7; 36; JOS 17:3-7
entry_id:
-1. Putra Lamekh KEJADIAN 5:28,29
     -Membangun bahtera (kapal) dan menyelamatkan keluarganya dari banjir besar KEJADIAN 6:14-22; 7; 8; MAT 24:38; Luk 17:27; IBR 11:7; 1Pe 3:20
     -Membangun altar dan mempersembahkan korban GEN 8:20,21
     -Menerima perjanjian dari Tuhan bahwa tidak akan ada banjir lagi yang melanda bumi; pelangi ditetapkan sebagai tanda perjanjian KEJADIAN 8:20,22; 9:9-17
     -Mabuk, dan kutukannya atas Kanaan GEN 9:20-27
     -Berkat-Nya atas Sem dan Yafet GEN 9:26,27
     -Meninggal pada umur sembilan ratus lima puluh tahun GEN 9:28,29
-2. Putri Zelafehad, peraturan khusus sehubungan dengan warisan Bil 26:33; 27:1-7; 36; Yos 17:3-7

subject: OATH
entry:
-(A solemn qualification) -Used in solemnizing covenants
     -Between Abraham and the king of Sodom GEN 14:22,23
     -Between Abraham and Abimelech GEN 21:22,23
     -Between Isaac and Abimelech GEN 26:26-29,31
-Abraham requires oath of his servant Eliezer GEN 24:2,3,9
-Esau confirms the sale of his birthright by GEN 25:33
-Jacob confirms the covenant between him and Laban by GEN 31:53
-Requires Joseph to swear that he would bury him with his forefathers GEN 47:28-31
-Joseph requires a similar oath GEN 50:25
-Rahab requires an oath from the spies JOS 2:12-14; 6:22
-The Israelites confirm the covenant with the Hivites JOS 9:3-20
-Moses covenants with Caleb by JOS 14:9
-The elders of Gilead confirm their pledge to Jephthah by JDG 11:10
-The Israelites swear in Mizpeh JDG 21:5
-Ruth swears to Naomi RUT 1:17
-Boaz swears to Ruth RUT 3:13
-Saul swears to Jonathan 1SA 19:6
-Jonathan and David confirm a covenant by 1SA 20:3,13-17
-David swears to Saul 1SA 24:21,22; 2SA 21:7
-Saul swears to the witch of En-dor 1SA 28:10
-David swears not to eat until the sun goes down 2SA 3:35
-Joab confirms his word by 2SA 19:7
-David swears to Bath-sheba that Solomon will be king 1KI 1:28,29
-Solomon confirms his word by 1KI 2:23
-So does Shimei 1KI 2:42
-Elisha seals his vow to follow Elijah by 2KI 2:2
-King of Samaria confirms his word with an 2KI 6:31
-Gehazi confirms his lie by 2KI 5:20

subject: PAUL
entry:
-Also called SAUL ACT 8:1; 9:1; 13:9
-From the tribe of Benjamin, ROM 11:1; PHP 3:5
-Personal appearance of 2CO 10:1,10; 11:6
-Born in the city of Tarsus ACT 9:11; 21:39; 22:3
-Educated at Jerusalem in the school of Gamaliel ACT 22:3; 26:4
-A zealous Pharisee ACT 22:3; 23:6; 26:5; 2CO 11:22; GAL 1:14; PHP 3:5
-A Roman citizen ACT 16:37; 22:25-28
-Persecutes the Christians; present at, and gives consent to, the stoning of Stephen ACT 7:58; 8:1,3; 9:1; 22:4
-Sent to Damascus with letters for the arrest and return to Jerusalem of Christians ACT 9:1,2
-His vision and conversion ACT 9:3-22; 22:4-19; 26:9-15; 1CO 9:1; 15:8; GAL 1:13; 1TI 1:12,13
-Is immersed ACT 9:18; 22:16
-Called to be an apostle ACT 22:14-21; 26:16-18; ROM 1:1; 1CO 1:1; 9:1,2; 15:9; GAL 1:1,15,16; EPH 1:1; COL 1:1; 1TI 1:1; 2:7; 2TI 1:1,11; TIT 1:1,3
-Preaches in Damascus for the first time ACT 9:20,22
-Is persecuted by the Jews ACT 9:23,24
-Escapes by being let down from the wall in a basket; goes to Jerusalem ACT 9:25,26; GAL 1:18,19
-Received by the disciples in Jerusalem ACT 9:26-29
-Goes to Caesarea ACT 9:30; 18:22
-Sent to the Gentiles ACT 13:2,3,47,48; 22:17-21; ROM 11:13; 15:16; GAL 1:15-24
-Has Barnabas as his companion ACT 11:25,26
-Teaches at Antioch (in Syria) for one year ACT 11:26
-Conveys the contributions of the Christians in Antioch to the Christians in Jerusalem ACT 11:27-30
-Returns with the apostle John to Antioch (of Syria) ACT 12:25
-Visits Seleucia ACT 13:4
-Visits much of the island of Cyprus ACT 13:4
-Preaches at Salamis ACT 13:5
-Preaches at Paphos ACT 13:6
-Sergius Paulus, governor of the country, is a convert of ACT 13:7-12
-Contends with Elymas (Bar-Jesus) the sorcerer ACT 13:6-12
-Visits Perga in Pamphylia ACT 13:13
-John (Mark), a companion of, departs for Jerusalem ACT 13:13
-Visits Antioch (in Pisidia), and preaches in the synagogue ACT 13:14-41
-His message received gladly by the Gentiles ACT 13:42,49
-Persecuted and expelled ACT 13:50,51
-Visits Iconium, and preaches to the Jews and non-Jews; is persecuted; escapes to Lystra; goes to Derbe ACT 14:1-6
-Heals an immobile man ACT 14:8-10
-The people attempt to worship him ACT 14:11-18
-Is persecuted by certain Jews from Antioch and Iconium, and is stoned ACT 14:19; 2CO 11:25; 2TI 3:11
-Escapes to Derbe, where he preaches the gospel, and returns to Lystra, and to Iconium, and to Antioch, strengthens the souls of the disciples, exhorts them to continue in the faith, and helps to appoint elders ACT 14:19-23
-Re-visits Pisidia, Pamphylia, Perga, Attalia, and Antioch, in Syria, where he lived ACT 14:24-28
-Contends with the Judaizers against their circumcision "theology" ACT 15:1,2
-Refers the question of circumcision to the apostles and elders at Jerusalem ACT 15:2,4
-He declares to the apostles at Jerusalem the miracles and wonders God had performed among the Gentiles by them ACT 15:12
-Returns to Antioch, accompanied by Barnabas, Judas, and Silas, with letters to the Gentiles ACT 15:22,25
-Makes his second tour of the congregations ACT 15:36
-Chooses Silas as his companion, and passes through Syria and Cilicia, strengthening the congregations ACT 15:36-41
-Visits Lystra; circumcises Timothy ACT 16:1-5
-Goes through Phrygia and Galatia; is forbidden by the Holy Spirit to preach in Asia; visits Mysia; attempts to go to Bithynia, but is restrained by the Spirit ACT 16:6,7
-Goes to Troas, where he has a vision of a man saying, "Come over into Macedonia, and help us; "immediately proceeds to Macedonia ACT 16:8-10
-Visits Samothracia and Neapolis; comes to Philippi, the chief city of Macedonia; visits a place of prayer at the side of the river; preaches the word; the merchant, Lydia, from Thyatira, is converted and immersed ACT 16:11-15
-Reproves the soothsayer; causes the evil spirit to come out of the girl who practises divination ACT 16:16-18
-Persecuted, beaten, and cast into prison with Silas; sings songs of praise in the prison; an earthquake shakes the prison; he preaches to the alarmed jailer, who believes, and is immersed along with his household ACT 16:19-34
-Is released by the civil authorities on the grounds of his being a Roman citizen ACT 16:35-39; 2CO 6:5; 11:25; 1TH 2:2
-Is welcomed at the household of Lydia ACT 16:40
-Visits Amphipolis, Apollonia, and Thessalonica; preaches in the synagogue ACT 17:1-4
-Is persecuted ACT 17:5-9; 2TH 1:1-4
-Escapes to Berea by night; preaches in the synagogue; many honorable women and several men believe ACT 17:10-12
-Persecuted by the Jews who come from Thessalonica; is escorted by some of the brethren to Athens ACT 17:13-15
-Debates on Mars' Hill (at the meeting of the Areopagus Council) with Greeks ACT 17:16-34
-Visits Corinth; lives with Aquila and his wife, Priscilla (Prisca), who were tentmakers; joins in their trade ACT 18:1-3
-Reasons in the synagogue every Sabbath; is rejected by the Jews; turns to the Gentiles; makes his home with Justus; continues there for eighteen months, teaching the word of God ACT 18:4-11
-Persecuted by Jews, drawn before the deputy, charged with wicked lewdness; accusation dismissed; takes his leave after many days, and sails to Syria, accompanied by Aquila and Priscilla ACT 18:12-18
-Visits Ephesus, where he leaves Aquila and Priscilla; enters into a synagogue, where he reasons with the Jews; starts on his return trip to Jerusalem; visits Caesarea; crosses over the country of Galatia and Phrygia, strengthening the disciples ACT 18:18-23
-Returns to Ephesus; immerses in the name of the Lord Jesus, and lays his hands upon the disciples, who are baptized with the Holy Spirit; preaches in the synagogue; remains in Ephesus for two years; heals the sick people ACT 19:12
-Rebukes the exorcists; casts an evil spirit out of a man, and many believe, bringing their evil books of sorcery to be burned ACT 19:13-20; 1CO 16:8,9
-Sends Timothy and Erastus into Macedonia, but he himself remains in Asia for a period of time ACT 19:21,22
-The spread of the gospel through his preaching interferes with the makers of idols; he is persecuted, and a great uproar of the city is created; the town clerk appeases the people; dismisses the accusation against Paul, and disperses the people ACT 19:23-41; 2CO 1:8; 2TI 4:14
-Proceeds to Macedonia after strengthening the congregations in that region; comes into Greece and lives for three months; returns through Macedonia, accompanied by Sopater, Aristarchus, Secundus, Gaius, Timothy, Tychicus, and Trophimus ACT 20:1-6
-Visits Troas; preaches until daybreak; restores to life the young man (Eutychus) who fell from the window ACT 20:6-12
-Visits Assos, Mitylene, Chios, Samos, Trogyllium, and Miletus, hastening to Jerusalem, to be there by Pentecost day ACT 20:13-16
-Sends for the elders of the congregation of Ephesus; relates to them how he had preached in Asia and his temptations and afflictions, urging repentance toward God ACT 20:17-21
-Declares he was going bound in spirit to Jerusalem; exhorts them to take heed to themselves and the flock over whom the Holy Spirit had made them overseers; kneels down, prays, and leaves ACT 20:22-38
-Visits Coos, Rhodes, and Patara; boards a ship bound for Tyre ACT 21:1-3
-Waits at Tyre for seven days; is brought on his way by the disciples to the outskirts of the city; kneels down and prays; boards the ship; comes to Ptolemais; greets the brethren, and stays for one day ACT 21:4-7
-Departs for Caesarea; enters the house of Philip the evangelist; is admonished by the prophet Agabus not to go to Jerusalem; nevertheless, he proceeds to Jerusalem ACT 21:8-15
-Is received by the brethren gladly; talks about the things that had been done among the Gentiles through his ministry ACT 21:17-25
-Enters the temple courtyard; the people are stirred up against him by some Jews from Asia; an uproar is created; he is thrust out of the temple area; the commander of the Roman garrison intervenes and arrests him ACT 21:26-33
-His defense ACT 21:33-40; 22:1-21
-Is confined in the fortress ACT 22:24-30
-Is brought before the Sanhedrin; his defense ACT 22:30; 23:1-5
-Is returned to the fortress ACT 23:10
-Is encouraged by a vision from God, promising him that he will give testimony in Rome ACT 23:11
-Jewish leaders conspire against his life ACT 23:12-15
-This plan is thwarted by his nephew ACT 23:16-22
-Is escorted to Caesarea by a military guard ACT 23:23-33
-Is confined in Herod's Judgment Hall in Caesarea ACT 23:35
-His trial before Governor Felix ACT 24
-Remains in custody for two years ACT 24:27
-His trial before Governor Festus ACT 25:1-12
-Appeals to be heard by Caesar ACT 25:10-12
-His examination before Herod Agrippa II ACT 25:13-27; 26
-Is taken to Rome in the custody of Julius, a centurion, and a detachment of soldiers; boards a ship, accompanied by other prisoners, and sails by way of the coasts of Asia; stops at Sidon, and at Myra ACT 27:1-5
-Transferred to a ship of Alexandria; sails by way of Cnidus, Crete, Salamis, and the Fair Havens ACT 27:6-8
-Predicts misfortune to the ship; his counsel not heeded, and the voyage resumes ACT 27:9-13
-The ship encounters a storm; Paul encourages and comforts the officers and crew; the soldiers advise putting the prisoners to death; the centurion interferes, and all on board (consisting of two-hundred and seventy-six persons) survive ACT 27:14-44
-The ship is wrecked, and all on board take refuge on the island of Melita (Malta) ACT 27:14-44
-Kind treatment by the inhabitants of the island ACT 28:1,2
-Is bitten by a viper and miraculously unharmed ACT 28:3-6
-Heals the ruler's father and others ACT 28:7-10
-Is delayed in Melita (Malta) for three months; proceeds on the voyage; delays at Syracuse; sails by Rhegium and Puteoli ACT 28:11-13
-Meets some brethren who accompany him to Rome from Appii Forum; arrives at Rome; is delivered to the captain of the guard; is permitted to live by himself in custody of a soldier ACT 28:14-16
-Summons the local Jewish leadership; states his position; is kindly received; expounds the gospel; testifies to the kingdom of heaven ACT 28:17-29
-Lives in his own rented house for two years, preaching and teaching ACT 28:30,31
-Supports himself ACT 18:3; 20:33-35
-Sickness of, in Asia 2CO 1:8-11
-His resolute determination to go to Jerusalem despite repeated warnings ACT 20:22,23; 21:4,10-14
-Caught up to the third heaven 2CO 12:1-4
-Has "a thorn in the flesh," 2CO 12:7-9; GAL 4:13,14
-His independence of character 1TH 2:9; 2TH 3:8
-Persecutions of 1TH 2:2; HEB 10:34
-Persecutions endured by, see below

subject: PETER
entry:
-Also called SIMON BAR-JONA and CEPHAS MAT 16:16-19; MRK 3:16; JHN 1:42
-A fisherman MAT 4:18; LUK 5:1-7; JHN 21:3
-Call of MAT 4:18-20; MRK 1:16-18; LUK 5:1-11
-His mother-in-law healed MAT 8:14; MRK 1:29,30; LUK 4:38
-An apostle MAT 10:2; 16:18,19; MRK 3:16; LUK 6:14; ACT 1:13
-An evangelist MRK 1:36,37
-Confesses Jesus to be the Messiah MAT 16:16-19; MRK 8:29; LUK 9:20; JHN 6:68,69
-His presumption
     -In rebuking Jesus MAT 16:22,23; MRK 8:32,33
     -When the throng was pressing Jesus and the woman with the blood disorder touched him LUK 8:45
     -In refusing to let Jesus wash Peter's feet JHN 13:6-11
-Present
     -At the healing of Jairus' daughter MRK 5:37; LUK 8:51
     -At the transfiguration MAT 17:1-4; MRK 9:2-6; LUK 9:28-33; 2PE 1:16-18
     -In the garden of Gethsemane MAT 26:36-46; MRK 14:33-42; LUK 22:40-46
-Seeks the interpretation -Of the parable of the steward LUK 12:41
-Of the law of forgiveness MAT 18:21
-Of the law of defilement MAT 15:15
-Of the prophecy of Jesus concerning his second coming MRK 13:3,4
-Walks upon the water of Lake Galilee MAT 14:28-31
-Sent with John to prepare the Passover meal LUK 22:8
-Calls attention to the withered fig tree MRK 11:21
-His treachery foretold by Jesus, and his profession of fidelity MAT 26:33-35; MRK 14:29-31; LUK 22:31-34; JHN 13:36-38
-Cuts off the ear of Malchus MAT 26:51; MRK 14:47; LUK 22:50
-Follows Jesus to the high priest's palace MAT 26:58; MRK 14:54; LUK 22:54; JHN 18:15
-His denial of Jesus, and his repentance MAT 26:69-75; MRK 14:66-72; LUK 22:55-62; JHN 18:17,18,25-27
-Visits the gravesite of Jesus LUK 24:12; JHN 20:2-6
-Jesus sends message to, after the resurrection MRK 16:7
-Jesus appears to LUK 24:34; 1CO 15:4,5
-Present at Lake Tiberias when Jesus appeared to his disciples; jumps into the water, and comes to shore when Jesus is recognized; is commissioned to feed the flock of Christ JHN 21:1-23
-Lives in Jerusalem ACT 1:13
-His statement in front of the disciples concerning the death of Judas, and his recommendation that the vacancy in the apostleship be filled ACT 1:15-22
-Preaches on Pentecost day ACT 2:14-40
-Heals the immobile man in the portico of the temple ACT 3
-Accused by the council; his defense ACT 4:1-23
-Foretells the death of Ananias and Sapphira ACT 5:1-11
-Imprisoned and scourged; his defense before the council ACT 5:17-42
-Goes to Samaria ACT 8:14
-Prays for the reception of the miraculous gifts of the Holy Spirit ACT 8:15-18
-Rebukes Simon, the sorcerer, who desires to purchase this power ACT 8:18-24
-Returns to Jerusalem ACT 8:25
-Receives Paul GAL 1:18; 2:9
-Visits Lydda; heals Aeneas ACT 9:32-34
-Visits Joppa; stays with Simon, the tanner; raises Dorcas from the dead ACT 9:36-43
-Has a vision of a sheet containing ceremonially clean and unclean animals ACT 10:9-16
-Receives the servant of the centurion; goes to Caesarea; preaches and immerses the centurion and his household ACT 10
-Advocates the preaching of the gospel to the Gentiles in the hearing of the apostles and elders ACT 11:1-18; 15:7-11
-Imprisoned and delivered by an angel ACT 12:3-19
-Writes two epistles 1PE 1:1; 2PE 1:1
-Miracles of
     -See MIRACLES

subject: PHILIP
entry:
-1. The brother of Herod Antipas and the husband of Herodias MAT 14:3; MRK 6:17; LUK 3:19
-2. Tetrarch of Iturea LUK 3:1
-3. One of the seven servants (Greek: diakonos) ACT 6:5
     -Successfully preaches in Samaria ACT 8:4-14
     -Expounds the Scriptures to the Ethiopian eunuch whom he immerses ACT 8:27-38
     -Caught away by the Spirit to Azotus, preaches in the cities, and goes to Caesarea ACT 8:39,40
     -Lives at Caesarea, and entertains Paul ACT 21:8
     -Has four daughters (prophetesses) ACT 21:9,10
-4. One of the twelve apostles MAT 10:3; MRK 3:18; LUK 6:14; ACT 1:13
     -Call of JHN 1:43
     -Brings Nathanael to Jesus JHN 1:45-50
     -Assists in caring for the multitude whom Jesus miraculously feeds JHN 6:5-7
     -Brings certain Greeks to Jesus who desire to see him JHN 12:20-22
     -Asks Jesus to show the Father JHN 14:8-13
entry_id:
-1. Saudara laki-laki Herodes Antipas dan suami Herodias MAT 14:3; Mrk 6:17; Lukas 3:19
-2. Raja Wilayah Iturea LUK 3:1
-3. Salah satu dari tujuh hamba (Yunani: diakonos) ACT 6:5
     -Sukses berdakwah di Samaria ACT 8:4-14
     -Memjelaskan Kitab Suci kepada sida-sida Etiopia yang dibenamkannya ACT 8:27-38
     -Dibawa Roh ke Azotus, berkhotbah di kota-kota, dan pergi ke Kaisarea ACT 8:39,40
     -Tinggal di Kaisarea, dan menghibur Paulus ACT 21:8
     -Memiliki empat orang putri (nabi) ACT 21:9,10
-4. Salah satu dari dua belas rasul MAT 10:3; Mrk 3:18; Lukas 6:14; TINDAKAN 1:13
     -Panggilan Yohanes 1:43
     -Membawa Natanael kepada Yesus JHN 1:45-50
     -Membantu merawat orang banyak yang diberi makan secara ajaib oleh Yesus JHN 6:5-7
     -Membawa orang Yunani tertentu kepada Yesus yang ingin bertemu dengannya JHN 12:20-22
     -Meminta Yesus menunjukkan kepada Bapa JHN 14:8-13

subject: PORTERS
entry:
-Guards at the city gates, the doors of the king' s palace, and doors of the temple 1CH 9:17-32; 2CH 34:13; 35:15
-Lodged round about the temple in order to be present for opening the doors 1CH 9:27
-One-third were porters of the temple 2CH 23:4
-One-third were porters of the king' s house 2CH 23:5
-One-third were porters of the gate of the foundation 2CH 23:5
-They also served as porters of the gates of the walls NEH 12:25
-They served in twenty-four courses 1CH 26:13-19
-Their posts were determined by lot 1CH 24:31; 26:13-19
entry_id:
-Penjaga di gerbang kota, pintu istana raja, dan pintu kuil 1CH 9:17-32; 2Taw 34:13; 35:15

-Sepertiganya adalah kuli angkut Bait Suci 2CH 23:4
-Sepertiganya adalah kuli di rumah raja 2CH 23:5
-Sepertiganya adalah penjaga pintu gerbang pondasi 2CH 23:5
-Mereka juga bertugas sebagai kuli di gerbang tembok NEH 12:25
-Mereka melayani dalam dua puluh empat kursus 1CH 26:13-19
-Pos mereka ditentukan dengan undian 1CH 24:31; 26:13-19

subject: POUND
entry:
-The Hebrew word "maneh" is translated "pound," 1KI 10:17; EZR 2:69; NEH 7:71,72
-And is equivalent to almost one pound (fourteen ounces)
-In John the weight was equivalent to about twelve ounces JHN 12:3
-In Luke the Greek word "mina" is translated "pound," and worth approximately one-hundred denarii (more than three months' wages) LUK 19:13-25
-See MEASURE
-See WEIGHTS
entry_id:
-Kata Ibrani "maneh" diterjemahkan "pon", 1KI 10:17; EZR 2:69; NEH 7:71,72
-Dan setara dengan hampir satu pon (empat belas ons)
-Dalam Yohanes beratnya setara dengan sekitar dua belas ons JHN 12:3
-Dalam Lukas, kata Yunani “mina” diterjemahkan “pound,” dan nilainya kira-kira seratus dinar (lebih dari upah tiga bulan) LUK 19:13-25
-Lihat UKURAN
-Lihat BERAT

subject: PUNISHMENT
entry:
-DEATH PENALTY
     -Will not be commuted NUM 35:31
     -In the Mosaic law the death penalty was inflicted
     -For murder GEN 9:5,6; NUM 35:16-21,30-33; DEU 17:6
     -For adultery LEV 20:10; DEU 22:24
     -For incest LEV 20:11,12,14
     -For bestiality EXO 22:19; LEV 20:15,16
     -For sodomy LEV 18:22; 20:13
     -For sexual immorality DEU 22:21-24
     -For the rape of a betrothed (engaged) virgin DEU 22:25
     -For perjury ZEC 5:4
     -For kidnapping EXO 21:16; DEU 24:7
     -For the promiscuousness of a priest' s daughter LEV 21:9
     -For witchcraft EXO 22:18
     -For offering human sacrifice LEV 20:2-5
     -For striking or cursing one' s father or mother EXO 21:15,17; LEV 20:9
     -For disobedience to parents DEU 21:18-21
     -For stealing ZEC 5:3,4
     -For blasphemy LEV 24:11-14,16,23
     -For desecrating the Sabbath day EXO 35:2; NUM 15:32-36
     -For prophesying falsely, or propagating false doctrines DEU 13:1-10
     -For sacrificing to false gods EXO 22:20
     -For refusing to abide by the decision of the court DEU 17:12
     -For treason 1KI 2:25; EST 2:23
     -For sedition ACT 5:36,37
     -MODES OF EXECUTION OF DEATH PENALTY
     -Burning GEN 38:24; LEV 20:14; 21:9; JER 29:22; EZK 23:25; DAN 3:19-23
     -Stoning LEV 20:2,27; 24:14; NUM 14:10; 15:33-36; DEU 13:10; 17:5; 22:21,24; JOS 7:25; 1KI 21:10; EZK 16:40
     -Hanging GEN 40:22; DEU 21:22,23; JOS 8:29
     -Beheading MAT 14:10; MRK 6:16,27,28
     -Crucifixion MAT 27:35,38; MRK 15:24,27; LUK 23:33
     -The sword EXO 32:27,28; 1KI 2:25,34,46; ACT 12:2
     -Executed by the accusing witnesses DEU 13:9; 17:7; ACT 7:58
     -Executed by the entire congregation NUM 15:35,36; DEU 13:9
     -Not inflicted by the testimony of less than two witnesses NUM 35:30; DEU 17:6; 19:15
-MINOR OFFENSES
     -Punishable by
     -Scourging LEV 19:20; DEU 22:18; 25:2,3; PRO 17:10; 19:29; 20:30; MAT 27:26; MRK 15:15; LUK 23:16; JHN 19:1; ACT 22:24,29

subject: RAAMAH
entry:
-1. Son of Cush GEN 10:7; 1CH 1:9
-2. A place in Arabia EZK 27:22
entry_id:
-1. Putra Kush KEJADIAN 10:7; 1CH 1:9
-2. Suatu tempat di Arabia EZK 27:22

subject: RAAMIAH
entry:
-One of those who returned to Jerusalem from captivity in Babylon NEH 7:7
-Called REELAIAH in EZR 2:2
entry_id:
-Salah satu dari mereka yang kembali ke Yerusalem dari pembuangan di Babel NEH 7:7
-Disebut REELAIAH dalam EZR 2:2

subject: RAAMSES
entry: -See RAMESES
entry_id: -Lihat RAMES

subject: RABBAH
entry:
-1. Also called RABBATH
     -A city east of the Jordan River, originally belonging to the Ammonites JOS 13:25
     -Bedstead of the giant named Og, kept at DEU 3:11
     -Captured by David 2SA 11:1; 12:26-31; 1CH 20:1-3
     -Possessed again by the Ammonites; prophesied against JER 49:2,3; EZK 21:20; 25:5; AMO 1:14
-2. A city in the territory of the tribe of Judah JOS 15:60
entry_id:
-1. Disebut juga RABBAT
     -Sebuah kota di sebelah timur Sungai Yordan, awalnya milik orang Amon JOS 13:25
     -Tempat tidur raksasa bernama Og, disimpan di DEU 3:11
     -Ditangkap oleh Daud 2SA 11:1; 12:26-31; 1Taw 20:1-3
     -Dimiliki lagi oleh orang Amon; bernubuat bertentangan dengan YER 49:2,3; EZK 21:20; 25:5; AMO 1:14
-2. Sebuah kota di wilayah suku Yehuda JOS 15:60

subject: RABBATH
entry: -See RABBAH
entry_id: -Lihat RABBAH

subject: RABBI
entry:
-(Literally, the Hebrew word means "my great one.")
-The title of a Jewish teacher MAT 23:7,8; JHN 3:2
-Ostentatiously used by the Pharisees MAT 23:7
-Used in addressing John the Baptist JHN 3:26
-Used in addressing Jesus JHN 1:38,49; 3:2; 6:25
-R. V MAT 26:25,49; MRK 9:5; 11:21; 14:45; JHN 4:31; 9:2; 11:8
-Jesus called "Rabboni," MRK 10:51
-R. V.) JHN 20:16
-Forbidden by Jesus as a title to his disciples MAT 23:8
entry_id:
-(Secara harfiah, kata Ibrani berarti "yang agung.")
-Gelar guru Yahudi MAT 23:7,8; Yohanes 3:2
-Digunakan secara mencolok oleh orang Farisi MAT 23:7
-Digunakan dalam menangani Yohanes Pembaptis JHN 3:26
-Digunakan dalam menyapa Yesus JHN 1:38,49; 3:2; 6:25
-R. V MAT 26:25,49; Mrk 9:5; 11:21; 14:45; Yohanes 4:31; 9:2; 11:8
-Yesus memanggil "Rabboni," MRK 10:51
-R. V.) Yohanes 20:16
-Dilarang oleh Yesus sebagai gelar kepada murid-muridnya MAT 23:8

subject: RABBITH
entry: -A city in the territory of the tribe of Issachar JOS 19:20
entry_id: -Sebuah kota di wilayah suku Isakhar JOS 19:20

subject: RAB-MAG
entry: -An Assyrian prince, or, possibly, a second name given to Nergal-sharezer JER 39:3,13
entry_id: -Seorang pangeran Asiria, atau, mungkin, nama kedua yang diberikan kepada Nergal-sharezer JER 39:3,13

subject: RABSARIS
entry:
-1. A title of an Assyrian officer
     -See RAB-SHAKEH (RABSHAKEH)
     -See TARTAN
     -Sent by Sennacherib against Jerusalem 2KI 18:17
-2. An Assyrian prince during the time of Nebuchadnezzar, or, possibly, a second name given to Nebushazban (also spelled NEBO-SARSEKIM or SARSEKIM, N. I. V.) JER 39:3,13
entry_id:
-1. Gelar perwira Asiria
     -Lihat RAB-SYAKEH (RABSYAKEH)
     -Lihat TARTAN
     -Dikirim oleh Sanherib terhadap Yerusalem 2KI 18:17
-2. Seorang pangeran Asiria pada masa Nebukadnezar, atau, mungkin, nama kedua yang diberikan kepada Nebushazban (juga dieja NEBO-SARSEKIM atau SARSEKIM, N.I.V.) JER 39:3,13

subject: RAB-SHAKEH (RABSHAKEH)
entry:
-(A title of an Assyrian officer)
-Sent by Sennacherib against Jerusalem; undertakes to cause disloyalty to Hezekiah and the surrender of Jerusalem by a speech in the Jews' native language 2KI 18:17-36; 19:4,8; ISA 36; 37
entry_id:
-(Gelar perwira Asiria)
-Dikirim oleh Sanherib melawan Yerusalem; berjanji untuk menyebabkan ketidaksetiaan kepada Hizkia dan penyerahan Yerusalem dengan pidato dalam bahasa asli Yahudi 2KI 18:17-36; 19:4,8; SA 36; 37

subject: RACA
entry: -(Roughly translated "fool" but means "You good-for-nothing moron!")) MAT 5:22
entry_id: -(Diterjemahkan secara kasar "bodoh" tetapi berarti "Dasar tolol yang tidak berguna!")) MAT 5:22

subject: RACE
entry:
-1. Human
     -Unity of GEN 3:20; MAL 2:10
-2. FOOT RACE
     -Figurative PSA 19:5; ECC 9:11; 1CO 9:24; GAL 5:7; PHP 2:16; HEB 12:1,2
entry_id:
-1. Manusia
     -Kesatuan GEN 3:20; MAL 2:10
-2. LOMBA KAKI
     -PSA figuratif 19:5; PAK 9:11; 1CO 9:24; GAL 5:7; PHP 2:16; IBR 12:1,2

subject: RACHAB
entry: -See RAHAB
entry_id: -Lihat RAHAB

subject: RACHAL
entry: -A city in the territory of the tribe of Judah 1SA 30:29
entry_id: -Sebuah kota di wilayah suku Yehuda 1SA 30:29

subject: RACHEL
entry:
-(Daughter of Laban and the favorite wife of Jacob)
-Meets Jacob at a well GEN 29:9-12
-Jacob serves Laban for an additional seven years to obtain her as his wife GEN 29:15-30
-Sterility of GEN 29:31
-Her grief in consequence of her sterility; gives her maid, to Jacob in order to obtain children in her own name GEN 30:1-8,15,22-34
-Later productiveness of; becomes the mother of
     -Joseph GEN 30:22-25
     -Benjamin GEN 35:16-18,24
-Steals the household images (teraphim, legal deed) of her father GEN 31:4,14-19,33-35
-Her death and burial GEN 35:18-20; 48:7; 1SA 10:2
entry_id:
-(Putri Laban dan istri kesayangan Yakub)
-Bertemu Yakub di sumur KEJADIAN 29:9-12
-Jacob melayani Laban selama tujuh tahun tambahan untuk mendapatkan dia sebagai istrinya GEN 29:15-30
-Sterilitas GEN 29:31
-Kesedihannya karena kemandulannya; memberikan pembantunya kepada Yakub agar memperoleh anak atas namanya sendiri KEJADIAN 30:1-8,15,22-34
-Produktivitas selanjutnya; menjadi ibu dari
     -Yusuf KEJADIAN 30:22-25
     -Benyamin KEJADIAN 35:16-18,24
-Mencuri gambar rumah tangga (terafim, akta hukum) ayahnya GEN 31:4,14-19,33-35
-Kematian dan penguburannya KEJADIAN 35:18-20; 48:7; 1SA 10:2

subject: RADDAI
entry: -Son of Jesse 1CH 2:14
entry_id: -Putra Isai 1CH 2:14

subject: RAGAU
entry:
-Also called REU
-Son of Peleg and an ancestor of Jesus GEN 11:18-21; 1CH 1:25; LUK 3:35
entry_id:
-Juga disebut REU
-Putra Peleg dan nenek moyang Yesus GEN 11:18-21; 1Taw 1:25; Lukas 3:35

subject: RAGUEL
entry:
-Also called REUEL and JETHRO
-Moses' father-in-law NUM 10:29
-See JETHRO
entry_id:
-Juga disebut REUEL dan JETHRO
-Mertua Musa NUM 10:29
-Lihat JETRO

subject: RAHAB
entry:
-1. Also called RACHAB
-(A woman of Jericho)
     -Assists the spies of the Israelites JOS 2
     -Is spared when the Israelites captured Jericho JOS 6:17-25
     -An ancestor of Joseph of Nazareth MAT 1:5
     -The faith of, commended HEB 11:31; JAS 2:25
-2. A symbolic name applied to Egypt PSA 87:4; 89:10; ISA 30:7; 51:9
entry_id:
-1. Disebut juga RACHAB
-(Seorang wanita Yerikho)
     -Membantu mata-mata bangsa Israel JOS 2
     -Terhindar ketika bangsa Israel merebut Yerikho JOS 6:17-25
     -Nenek moyang Yusuf dari Nazareth MAT 1:5
     -Iman, dipuji IBR 11:31; Yak 2:25
-2. Sebuah nama simbolis yang diterapkan pada Mesir PSA 87:4; 89:10; ISA 30:7; 51:9

subject: RAHAM
entry: -Son of Shema 1CH 2:44
entry_id: -Anak Shema 1CH 2:44

subject: RAMAH
entry:
-1. Called RAMA MAT 2:18
     -A city allotted to the tribe of Benjamin JOS 18:25; JDG 19:13
     -Attempted fortification of, by King Baasha; destruction of, by Asa 1KI 15:17-22; 2CH 16:1-6
     -People of, return from the Babylonian captivity EZR 2:26; NEH 7:30; 11:33
     -Jeremiah imprisoned in JER 40:1
     -Prophecies concerning ISA 10:29; JER 31:15; HOS 5:8; MAT 2:18
-2. A city of the territory of the tribe of Asher JOS 19:29
-3. A city of the territory of the tribe of Naphthali JOS 19:36
-4. Also called RAMATHAIM-ZOPHIM
     -A city near Mount Ephraim Jude 1:4,5; 1SA 1:1
     -Home of Elkanah 1SA 1:1,19; 2:11
     -Home of Samuel 1SA 1:19,20; 7:17; 8:4; 15:34; 16:13
     -David flees to 1SA 19:18
     -Samuel dies and was buried in 1SA 25:1; 28:3
-5. RAMAH
     -See RAMOTH-GILEAD
entry_id:
-1. Disebut RAMA MAT 2:18
     -Sebuah kota yang diberikan kepada suku Benyamin JOS 18:25; Hak 19:13
     -Upaya benteng, oleh Raja Baasha; kehancuran, menurut Asa 1KI 15:17-22; 2Taw 16:1-6
     -Orang-orang yang kembali dari pembuangan di Babilonia EZR 2:26; NEH 7:30; 11:33
     -Yeremia dipenjarakan dalam JER 40:1
     -Nubuatan tentang ISA 10:29; Yer 31:15; HOS 5:8; MAT 2:18
-2. Sebuah kota di wilayah suku Asyer JOS 19:29
-3. Kota di wilayah suku Naftali JOS 19:36
-4. Disebut juga RAMATHAIM-ZOPHIM
     -Sebuah kota dekat Gunung Efraim Yudas 1:4,5; 1SA 1:1
     -Rumah Elkana 1SA 1:1,19; 2:11
     -Rumah Samuel 1SA 1:19,20; 7:17; 8:4; 15:34; 16:13
     -David melarikan diri ke 1SA 19:18
     -Samuel meninggal dan dikuburkan dalam 1SA 25:1; 28:3
-5. RAMAH
     -Lihat RAMOTH-GILEAD

subject: RAMOTH-GILEAD
entry:
-Also called RAMAH 2KI 8:2; 2CH 22:6
-A city of the territory of the tribe of Gad, and one of the cities of refuge DEU 4:43; JOS 20:8; 1CH 6:80
-One of Solomon' s commissaries there 1KI 4:13
-In the possession of the Syrians 1KI 22:3
-Besieged by Israel and Judah; Ahab killed there 1KI 22:29-36; 2CH 18
-Recovered by Joram; Joram wounded there 2KI 8:28,29; 9:14,15; 2CH 22:5,6
-Elisha anoints Jehu to be king there 2KI 9:1-6
entry_id:
-Disebut juga RAMAH 2KI 8:2; 2CH 22:6
-Sebuah kota di wilayah suku Gad, dan salah satu kota perlindungan DEU 4:43; Yos 20:8; 1CH 6:80
-Salah satu komisaris Sulaiman di sana 1KI 4:13
-Dalam kepemilikan orang Siria 1KI 22:3
-Dikepung oleh Israel dan Yehuda; Ahab dibunuh disana 1KI 22:29-36; 2CH 18
-Dipulihkan oleh Joram; Joram terluka disana 2KI 8:28,29; 9:14,15; 2CH 22:5,6
-Elisa mengurapi Yehu menjadi raja di sana 2KI 9:1-6

subject: SAUL
entry:
-1. Also called SHAUL
     -King of Edom GEN 36:37,38; 1CH 1:48,49
-2. King of Israel
     -A Benjamite, son of Kish 1SA 9:1,2
     -Sons of 1CH 8:33
     -His personal appearance 1SA 9:2; 10:23
     -Made king of Israel 1SA 9; 10; 11:12-15; HOS 13:11
     -Lives at Gibeah of Saul 1SA 14:2; 15:34; ISA 10:29
     -Defeats the Philistines 1SA 13; 14:46,52
     -Strikes the Amalekites 1SA 15
     -Is reproved by Samuel for usurping the priestly functions 1SA 13:11-14
     -Samuel rebukes him for disobedience by not exterminating the Amalekites; the loss of his kingdom is foretold 1SA 15
     -Dedicates the spoils of war 1SA 15:21-25; 1CH 26:28
     -Sends messengers to Jesse, asking that David be sent to him as a musician and an armor-bearer 1SA 16:17-23
     -Defeats the Philistines after Goliath is killed by David 1SA 17
     -His jealousy of David; gives his daughter, Michal, to David to be his wife; becomes David' s enemy 1SA 18
     -Tries to kill David; Jonathan intercedes and incurs his father' s displeasure; David' s loyalty to Jonathan; Saul' s temporary remorse; prophesies 1SA 19
     -Hears Doeg against Ahimelech, and kills the priest and his family. pursues David to wilderness of Ziph; the Ziphites betray David to 1SA 23
     -Pursues David to En-gedi 1SA 24:1-6
     -His life spared by David 1SA 24:5-8
     -Saul' s contrition for his bad faith 1SA 24:16-22
     -David is again betrayed to, by the Ziphites; Saul pursues him to the hill of Hachilah; his life spared again by David; his confession, and his blessing upon David 1SA 26
     -Kills the Gibeonites; crime avenged by the death of seven of his sons 2SA 21:1-9
     -His kingdom invaded by Philistines; seeks counsel of the witch of En-dor, who foretells his own death 1SA 28:3-25; 29:1
     -Is defeated, and is killed with some of his sons 1SA 31
     -Their bodies exposed in Beth-shan; rescued by the people of Jabesh and burned; bones of, buried under a tree at Jabesh 1SA 31; with 2SA 1; 2; 1CH 10
     -His death is a judgment on account of his sins 1CH 10:13
-3. Of Tarsus
     -See PAUL
entry_id:
-1. Disebut juga SHAUL
     -Raja Edom KEJADIAN 36:37,38; 1Taw 1:48,49
-2. Raja Israel
     -Seorang Benyamin, putra Kish 1SA 9:1,2
     -Putra 1CH 8:33
     -Penampilan pribadinya 1SA 9:2; 10:23
     -Dijadikan raja Israel 1SA 9; 10; 11:12-15; HOS 13:11
     -Tinggal di Gibeah Saul 1SA 14:2; 15:34; ISA 10:29
     -Kalahkan orang Filistin 1SA 13; 14:46,52
     -Menyerang orang Amalek 1SA 15
     -Ditegur oleh Samuel karena merampas fungsi imam 1SA 13:11-14
     -Samuel menegur dia karena ketidaktaatan dengan tidak memusnahkan orang Amalek; hilangnya kerajaannya dinubuatkan 1SA 15
     -Mempersembahkan rampasan perang 1SA 15:21-25; 1Taw 26:28
     -Mengirim utusan kepada Isai, meminta agar Daud diutus kepadanya sebagai pemusik dan pembawa senjata 1SA 16:17-23
     -Kalahkan orang Filistin setelah Goliat dibunuh oleh David 1SA 17
     -Kecemburuannya pada David; memberikan putrinya, Mikhal, kepada Daud untuk menjadi istrinya; menjadi musuh Daud 1SA 18
     -Mencoba membunuh David; Jonathan menjadi perantara dan menimbulkan ketidaksenangan ayahnya; kesetiaan Daud kepada Yonatan; penyesalan Saul yang sementara; bernubuat 1SA 19
     -Mendengar Doeg melawan Ahimelekh, dan membunuh pendeta dan keluarganya. mengejar Daud sampai ke padang gurun Zif; orang Zifit mengkhianati Daud ke 1SA 23
     -Mengejar Daud ke En-gedi 1SA 24:1-6
     -Hidupnya diselamatkan oleh Daud 1SA 24:5-8
     -Penyesalan Saul atas itikad buruknya 1SA 24:16-22
     -David kembali dikhianati oleh orang Zifit; Saul mengejarnya sampai ke bukit Hakhila; nyawanya diselamatkan lagi oleh David; pengakuannya, dan berkatnya atas Daud 1SA 26
     -Membunuh orang Gibeon; kejahatan yang dibalas dengan kematian tujuh putranya 2SA 21:1-9
     -Kerajaannya diserang oleh orang Filistin; mencari nasihat dari penyihir dari En-Dor, yang meramalkan kematiannya sendiri 1SA 28:3-25; 29:1
     -Dikalahkan, dan dibunuh bersama beberapa putranya 1SA 31
     -Tubuh mereka diekspos di Beth-shan; diselamatkan oleh penduduk Yabesh dan dibakar; tulang-tulangnya, dikubur di bawah pohon di Yabesh 1SA 31; dengan 2SA 1; 2; 1CH 10
     -Kematiannya adalah penghakiman karena dosanya 1CH 10:13
-3. Dari Tarsus
     -Lihat PAULUS

subject: SELF-DELUSION
entry:
-A characteristic of wicked people PSA 49:18
-Prosperity frequently leads to PSA 30:6; HOS 12:8; LUK 12:17-19
-Obstinate sinners often given up to PSA 81:11,12; HOS 4:17; 2TH 2:10,11
-Exhibited in thinking that
     -Our own ways are right PRO 14:12
     -We should adhere to established wicked practices JER 44:17
     -We are pure PRO 30:12
     -We are better than others LUK 18:11
     -We are rich in spiritual things REV 3:17
     -We may have peace while in sin DEU 29:19
     -We are above adversity PSA 10:6
     -Gifts entitle us to go to heaven MAT 7:21,22
     -Privileges entitle us to enter heaven MAT 3:9; LUK 13:25,26
     -God will not punish our sins JER 5:12
     -Christ will not come to condemn us 2PE 3:4
     -Our lives will be prolonged ISA 56:12; LUK 12:19; JAS 4:13
-Frequently persevered in, to the end MAT 7:22; 25:11,12; LUK 13:24,25
-Fatal consequences of MAT 7:23; 24:48-51; LUK 12:20; 1TH 5:3
-EXEMPLIFIED
     -Ahab 1KI 20:27,34
     -Israelites HOS 12:8
     -Jews JHN 8:33,41
     -The Laodicean congregation REV 3:17
     -See CONFIDENCE, FALSE
     -See SECURITY, FALSE
entry_id:
-Ciri-ciri orang fasik PSA 49:18
-Kemakmuran sering kali mengarah pada PSA 30:6; HOS 12:8; Lukas 12:17-19
-Orang berdosa yang keras kepala sering kali menyerah pada PSA 81:11,12; HOS 4:17; 2 2:10,11
-Dipamerkan dalam pemikiran itu
     -Cara kita sendiri benar PRO 14:12
     -Kita harus mengikuti praktik jahat yang sudah mapan JER 44:17
     -Kami murni PRO 30:12
     -Kami lebih baik dari yang lain LUK 18:11
     -Kita kaya akan hal-hal rohani Wahyu 3:17
     -Semoga kita mendapat kedamaian dalam dosa UL 29:19
     -Kita diatas kesulitan PSA 10:6
     -Hadiah membuat kita berhak masuk surga MAT 7:21,22
     -Keistimewaan memberi kita hak masuk surga MAT 3:9; Lukas 13:25,26
     -Tuhan tidak akan menghukum dosa kita JER 5:12
     -Kristus tidak akan datang untuk menghukum kita 2PE 3:4
     -Hidup kita akan diperpanjang ISA 56:12; Luk 12:19; Yak 4:13
-Sering bertahan, sampai akhir MAT 7:22; 25:11,12; Lukas 13:24,25
-Konsekuensi fatal dari MAT 7:23; 24:48-51; Lukas 12:20; 1 5:3
-CONTOH
     -Ahab 1KI 20:27,34
     -Orang Israel HOS 12:8
     -Yahudi Yohanes 8:33,41
     -Jemaat Laodikia REV 3:17

subject: SELF-DENIAL
entry:
-General scriptures concerning GEN 22:1-12; 2SA 24:24; PSA 132:3-5; PRO 16:32; 23:2; DAN 10:3; MAT 5:29,30; 8:19-22; 10:37-39; 13:44-46; 16:24,25; 18:8,9; 19:12,21; MRK 2:14; 8:34,35; 9:43; 10:29; 12:43,44; LUK 5:11,27; 9:23,24,57-60; 14:26-33; 18:27-30; 21:2-4; JHN 12:25; ACT 20:22-24; 21:13; ROM 6:6; 8:12,13,35,36; 13:14; 14:1-22; 15:1-5; 1CO 6:12; 8:10-13; 9:12,15,18,19,23,25-27; 10:23,24; 2CO 6:3; GAL 2:20; 5:16,17,24; 6:14; PHP 2:4; 3:7-9; COL 3:5; 2TI 2:4; TIT 2:12; HEB 13:13; 1PE 2:11-16; 4:1,2; 3JN 1:7; REV 12:11
-See CROSS
-See HUMILITY
-INSTANCES OF
     -Abraham, when he offered to Lot (his junior) his preference of the land of Canaan GEN 13:9; with 17:8
     -Moses, in choosing rather to suffer affliction with the people of God than to enjoy the pleasures of sin for only a little while HEB 11:25
     -Moses, in taking no compensation from the Israelites for his services NUM 16:15
     -Samuel, in his administration of justice 1SA 12:3,4
     -The widow of Zarephath, in sharing with Elijah the last of her sustenance 1KI 17:12-15
     -Daniel, in his abstention DAN 1:8
     -Daniel, in refusing rewards from Belshazzar DAN 5:16,17
     -Esther, in risking her life for the deliverance of her people EST 4:16
     -The Rechabites, in refusing to drink wine or strong drink, or even to plant vineyards JER 35:6,7
     -Peter and the other apostles, in abandoning their vocations and following Jesus MAT 4:20; 9:9; MRK 1:16-20; 2:14; LUK 5:11,27,28
     -in forsaking all and following Jesus MAT 19:27; MRK 10:28
     -The widow, who put everything she had into the treasury LUK 21:4
     -The early Christians, in having everything in common ACT 2:44,45; 4:34
     -Barnabas (Joses), in selling his possessions, and giving everything that he had received to the apostles ACT 4:36,37
     -Paul, in not regarding even his life dear to himself ACT 20:24; PHP 3:7,8
     -Paul, in not coveting anyone' s silver, gold, or apparel ACT 20:33

subject: SEVEN
entry:
-Interesting facts concerning this number
-DAYS
     -Week consists of GEN 2:3; EXO 20:11; DEU 5:13,14
     -Noah in the ark before the great flood for GEN 7:4,10
     -Noah remains in the ark after sending forth the dove for GEN 8:10,12
     -Mourning for Jacob lasted for GEN 50:10
     -The sympathy for JOB by Eliphaz, Bildad, and Zophar for JOB 2:13
     -The plague of bloody waters in Egypt lasted for EXO 7:25
     -The Israelites encircled Jericho for JOS 6:4
     -The Passover lasted for EXO 12:15
     -Saul directed by Samuel to stay at Gilgal awaiting the prophet' s command for 1SA 10:8; 13:8
     -The elders of Jabesh-gilead ask for a truce of 1SA 11:3
     -Dedication of the temple lasted double 1KI 8:65
     -Ezekiel sits by the Chebar River in astonishment for EZK 3:15
     -The Feast of Tabernacles lasted for LEV 23:34,42
     -The consecration of priests and altars lasted for EXO 29:30,35; EZK 43:25,26
     -Defilements lasted for LEV 12:2; 13:4
     -Fasts of 1SA 31:13; 2SA 12:16,18,22
     -The firstborn of flocks and sheep must remain with their mothers, before being offered EXO 22:30
     -The feast of Ahasuerus continued for EST 1:5
     -Paul waits at Tyre for ACT 21:4
     -Paul stays at Puteoli for ACT 28:14
-WEEKS
     -In Daniel' s vision concerning the coming of the Messiah for DAN 9:25
     -Ten times for DAN 9:24
     -The period between the Passover Feast and the Feast of Pentecost LEV 23:15
-MONTHS
     -Holy convocations in the seventh month LEV 23:24-44; NUM 29; EZK 45:25
-YEARS
     -Jacob serves for each of his wives for GEN 29:15-30
     -Of abundance for GEN 41:1-32,53
     -A famine lasted in Egypt for GEN 41:1-32,54-56
     -A famine lasted in Canaan for 2SA 24:13; 2KI 8:1
     -The insanity of Nebuchadnezzar for DAN 4:32
     -Seven times, the period between the jubilees LEV 25:8
-MISCELLANY OF SEVENS
     -Of the ceremonially clean creatures taken into Noah' s ark GEN 7:2
     -Abraham gives Abimelech seven lambs GEN 21:28
     -Rams and bullocks to the number of, required in sarifices LEV 23:18; NUM 23:1; 29:32; 1CH 15:26; EZK 45:23
     -The sprinkling of blood seven times LEV 4:6; 14:7
     -The sprinkling of oil seven times LEV 14:16
     -Seven cows and seven ears of grain in Pharaoh' s vision GEN 41:2-7
     -The Israelites surrounded Jericho seven times, and on the seventh day sounding seven trumpets JOS 6:4
     -Elisha' s servant looked seven times for the appearance of rain 1KI 18:43
     -Naaman was required to wash in the Jordan River seven times 2KI 5:10
     -The seven steps in the temple seen in Ezekiel' s vision EZK 40:22,26
     -The heat of Nebuchadnezzars furnace was intensified sevenfold DAN 3:19
     -The light of the sun was intensified sevenfold ISA 30:26
     -The threatened punishment of Israel was sevenfold LEV 26:18-21
     -Silver was purified seven times PSA 12:6
     -Worshiping seven times a day PSA 119:164
     -Seven chamberlains at the court of Ahasuerus EST 1:10
     -Seven princes EST 1:14
     -Seven counsellors at the court of Artaxerxes EZR 7:14
     -Seven maidens given to Esther EST 2:9
     -Symbolical
     -Of many sons RUT 4:15; 1SA 2:5; JER 15:9
     -Of liberality ECC 11:1,2
     -Seven magi (wise men) PRO 26:16
     -Seven women will seek a polyandrous marriage ISA 4:1
     -Seven shepherds to be sent forth against Assyria MIC 5:5,6
     -Seven lamps and pipes ZEC 4:2
     -Seven servants (Greek: diakonoi) in the Jerusalem congregations ACT 6:3
     -Seven congregations in Asia REV 1:4,20
     -Seven seals REV 5:1
     -Seven thunders REV 10:3
     -Seven heads and seven crowns REV 12:3; 13:1; 17:9
     -Seven kings REV 17:10
     -Seven stars REV 1:16,20; 3:1; AMO 5:8
     -Seven spirits REV 1:4; 3:1; 4:5; 5:6
     -Seven eyes of the Lord ZEC 3:9; 4:10; REV 5:6
     -Seven golden lampstands REV 1:12
     -Seven angels with seven trumpets REV 8:2
     -Seven plagues REV 15:1
     -Seven horns and seven eyes REV 5:6
     -Seven angels with seven plagues REV 15:6
     -Seven golden vials REV 15:7
     -The scarlet-colored beast having seven heads REV 17:3,7

subject: SHECHEM
entry:
-1. Also called SICHEM and SYCHEM, a district in the central part of the land of Canaan
     -Abraham lives in GEN 12:6
     -The flocks and herds of Jacob kept in GEN 37:12-14
     -Joseph buried in JOS 24:32
     -Jacob buried in ACT 7:16; with GEN 50:13
-2. Also called SYCHAR, a city of refuge in Mount Ephraim JOS 20:7; 21:21; JDG 21:19
     -Joshua assembled the tribes of Israel at, with all their elders, chiefs, and judges, and presented them before the Lord JOS 24:1-28
     -Joshua buried at JOS 24:30-32
     -Abimelech made king at JDG 8:31; 9
     -Rehoboam crowned at 1KI 12:1
     -Destroyed by Abimelech JDG 9:45
     -Rebuilt by Jeroboam 1KI 12:25
     -Men of, killed by Ishmael JER 41:5
     -Jesus visits; disciples made in JHN 4:1-42
-3. Son of Hamor; seduces Jacob' s daughter; killed by Jacob' s sons GEN 33:19; 34; JOS 24:32; JDG 9:28
     -Called SYCHEM ACT 7:16
-4. Ancestor of the Shechemites NUM 26:31; JOS 17:2
-5. Son of Shemidah 1CH 7:19
entry_id:
-1. Disebut juga SICHEM dan SYCHEM, sebuah distrik di bagian tengah tanah Kanaan
     -Abraham hidup dalam KEJADIAN 12:6
     -Kawanan domba dan lembu Yakub dipelihara dalam KEJADIAN 37:12-14
     -Yusuf dimakamkan di JOS 24:32
     -Yakub dikuburkan dalam ACT 7:16; dengan KEJADIAN 50:13
-2. Disebut juga SIKHAR, kota perlindungan di Gunung Efraim JOS 20:7; 21:21; Hak 21:19
     -Yosua mengumpulkan suku-suku Israel di situ dengan segenap tua-tua, pemimpin-pemimpin, dan hakim-hakim mereka, lalu menghadapkan mereka ke hadapan Tuhan JOS 24:1-28
     -Yosua dimakamkan pada JOS 24:30-32
     -Abimelekh diangkat menjadi raja di Hak 8:31; 9
     -Rehabeam dimahkotai pada 1KI 12:1
     -Dihancurkan oleh Abimelekh JDG 9:45
     -Dibangun kembali oleh Yerobeam 1KI 12:25
     -Pria, dibunuh oleh Ismael JER 41:5
     -Kunjungan Yesus; murid yang dibuat dalam Yohanes 4:1-42
-3. Putra Hamor; merayu putri Yakub; dibunuh oleh anak-anak Yakub KEJADIAN 33:19; 34; Yos 24:32; Hak 9:28
     -Disebut SYCHEM ACT 7:16
-4. Nenek moyang suku Sikhem NUM 26:31; Yos 17:2
-5. Putra Semida 1CH 7:19

subject: SIDON
entry:
-1. Also called ZIDON
     -Son of Canaan GEN 10:15; 1CH 1:13
-2. A city on the northern boundary of the Canaanites GEN 10:19
     -Designated by Jacob as the border of the tribe of Zebulun GEN 49:13
     -Was on the northern boundary of the tribe of Asher JOS 19:28; 2SA 24:6
     -Belonged to the land of Israel according to a promise JOS 13:6
     -Inhabitants of, lived in security and without a worry JDG 18:7
     -Israelites failed to make conquest of JDG 1:31; 3:3
     -The inhabitants of, contributed cedar for the first and second temple 1KI 5:6; 1CH 22:4; EZR 3:7
     -Solomon marries women of 1KI 11:1
     -Ahab marries a woman of 1KI 16:31
     -People of, come to hear Jesus MRK 3:8; LUK 6:17
     -Inhabitants of, offend Herod Agrippa I ACT 12:20-23
     -Commerce of ISA 23:2,4,12
     -The sailors of EZK 27:8
     -Prophecies concerning JER 25:15-22; 27:3-11; 47:4; EZK 28:21-23; 32:30; JOL 3:4-8
     -Jesus visits the region of, and heals the daughter of the non-Jewish, Syro-Phoenician woman MAT 15:21-28; MRK 7:24-31
     -Visited by Paul ACT 27:3
entry_id:
-1. Disebut juga ZIDON
     -Putra Kanaan KEJADIAN 10:15; 1Taw 1:13
-2. Sebuah kota di perbatasan utara bangsa Kanaan KEJADIAN 10:19
     -Ditunjuk oleh Yakub sebagai perbatasan suku Zebulon GEN 49:13
     -Berada di batas utara suku Asyer JOS 19:28; 2SA 24:6
     -Milik tanah Israel sesuai dengan janji JOS 13:6
     -Penduduknya, hidup aman dan tanpa rasa khawatir JDG 18:7
     -Bangsa Israel gagal melakukan penaklukan terhadap Hak 1:31; 3:3
     -Penduduk menyumbangkan kayu aras untuk kuil pertama dan kedua 1KI 5:6; 1Taw 22:4; EZR 3:7
     -Salomo menikahi wanita 1KI 11:1
     -Ahab menikah dengan wanita 1KI 16:31
     -Umat, datang mendengarkan Yesus MRK 3:8; Lukas 6:17
     -Penduduk, menyinggung Herodes Agripa I ACT 12:20-23
     -Perdagangan ISA 23:2,4,12
     -Para pelaut EZK 27:8
     -Nubuatan tentang YER 25:15-22; 27:3-11; 47:4; EZK 28:21-23; 32:30; Yoh 3:4-8
     -Yesus mengunjungi wilayah tersebut, dan menyembuhkan putri seorang wanita Siro-Fenisia non-Yahudi MAT 15:21-28; Mrk 7:24-31
     -Dikunjungi oleh Paulus ACT 27:3

     -On the plains of Moab NUM 26:14
     -Place of, in camping and marching NUM 2:12; 10:18,19
     -Inheritance allotted to JOS 19:1-9; JDG 1:3-17; 1CH 4:24-43
     -Stood on Mount Gerizim to bless at the time of the recounting of the law of Moses DEU 27:12
     -Joined with the people of Judah and Benjamin in the renewal of the Passover 2CH 15:9; with 15:1-15
     -Idolatry of 2CH 34:6; with 34:1-7
     -See ISRAEL
-3. A devout man in Jerusalem
     -Blesses Jesus (when an infant) in the temple LUK 2:25-35
-4. An ancestor of Jesus LUK 3:30
-5. A disciple
     -Also called NIGER ACT 13:1
-6. Name given to Peter ACT 15:14
     -See PETER
entry_id:
-1. Putra Yakub KEJADIAN 29:33; 35:23; Keluaran 1:1,2; 1CH 2:1
     -Dengan Lewi membalas dendam pada orang Sikhem rayuan Dinah GEN 34; 49:5-7
     -Kecaman Yakub terhadap KEJADIAN 34:30; 49:5-7
     -Pergi ke Mesir untuk membeli jagung (graini); dipenjarakan oleh Yusuf, dan ditahan KEJADIAN 42:24,36; 43:23
     -Anak-anaknya KEJADIAN 46:10; Keluaran 6:15; 1Taw 4:24-37
     -Keturunan NUM 26:12-14
     -Lihat SUKU, di bawah
-2. SUKU
     -Pendaftaran militer, di Sinai NUM 1:22,23; 2:13
     -Di dataran Moab NUM 26:14
     -Tempat berkemah dan berbaris NUM 2:12; 10:18,19
     -Warisan diberikan kepada JOS 19:1-9; Hak 1:3-17; 1Taw 4:24-43
     -Berdiri di gunung Gerizim untuk memberkati pada saat menceritakan hukum Musa DEU 27:12
     -Bergabung dengan masyarakat Yehuda dan Benyamin dalam pembaharuan Paskah 2CH 15:9; dengan 15:1-15
     -Penyembahan berhala di 2CH 34:6; dengan 34:1-7
     -Lihat ISRAEL

     -Memberkati Yesus (ketika masih bayi) di bait suci LUK 2:25-35
-4. Nenek moyang Yesus LUK 3:30
-5. Seorang murid
     -Juga disebut NIGER ACT 13:1
-6. Nama yang diberikan kepada Petrus ACT 15:14
     -Lihat PETER

subject: SIMON
entry:
-1. SIMON
     -See PETER
-2. One of the twelve apostles; a revolutionary and a patriot
     -Called "THE CANAANITE," (from the Hebrew root meaning "religious zeal" MAT 10:4; MRK 3:18
     -Called "ZELOTES," LUK 6:15; ACT 1:13
-3. A physical half-brother of Jesus MAT 13:55; MRK 6:3
-4. A leper
     -Jesus dines with MAT 26:6; MRK 14:3
-5. A man from the city of Cyrene
     -Compelled to carry Jesus' cross MAT 27:32; MRK 15:21; LUK 23:26
-6. A Pharisee
     -Jesus dines with LUK 7:36-44
-7. The father of Judas Iscariot JHN 6:71; 12:4; 13:2,26
-8. A sorcerer (also called Simon Magus)
     -Converted by Philip
     -Rebuked by Peter ACT 8:9-13,18-24
-9. A tanner
     -Peter lodges with ACT 9:43; 10:6,17,32
entry_id:
-1. SIMON
     -Lihat PETER
-2. Salah satu dari dua belas rasul; seorang revolusioner dan patriot
     -Disebut "ORANG KANAAN," (dari akar kata Ibrani yang berarti "semangat beragama" MAT 10:4; Mrk 3:18
     -Disebut "ZELOTES," LUK 6:15; TINDAKAN 1:13
-3. Saudara tiri Yesus secara fisik MAT 13:55; Mrk 6:3
-4. Seorang penderita kusta
     -Yesus makan dengan MAT 26:6; Mrk 14:3
-5. Seorang pria dari kota Kirene
     -Dipaksa memikul salib Yesus MAT 27:32; Mrk 15:21; Lukas 23:26
-6. Seorang Farisi
     -Yesus makan dengan LUK 7:36-44
-7. Ayah Yudas Iskariot JHN 6:71; 12:4; 13:2,26
-8. Seorang penyihir (juga disebut Simon Magus)
     -Dikonversi oleh Philip
     -Ditegur oleh Petrus ACT 8:9-13,18-24
-9. Seorang penyamak kulit
     -Petrus mengajukan dengan ACT 9:43; 10:6,17,32

subject: SODOMITES
entry:
-(The inhabitants of Sodom, many of whom openly practiced homosexuality)
-Wickedness of GEN 19:4-14
-Destroyed by fire as a judgement GEN 19:24,25
-To be judged according to opportunity MAT 11:24; LUK 10:12
-A proverbial term of reproach applied to those who practiced sodomy (ritual homosexuality) DEU 23:17; 1KI 14:24; 15:12; 22:46; 2KI 23:7; JOB 36:14
-The word "harlot" ("shrine prostitute" (N. I. V.)) appears in GEN 38:21,22
-And is applied to males DEU 23:17
-And is the translation of a Hebrew feminine form of the word translated elsewhere "sodomite" HOS 4:14
-See SODOMY
entry_id:
-(Penduduk Sodom, banyak di antaranya secara terbuka melakukan homoseksualitas)

-Dihancurkan oleh api sebagai penghakiman KEJ 19:24,25
-Untuk dinilai berdasarkan kesempatan MAT 11:24; Lukas 10:12
-Istilah celaan yang diterapkan pada mereka yang melakukan sodomi (ritual homoseksualitas) DEU 23:17; 1KI 14:24; 15:12; 22:46; 2KI 23:7; PEKERJAAN 36:14
-Kata "pelacur" ("pelacur kuil" (N.I.V.)) muncul dalam KEJADIAN 38:21,22
-Dan diterapkan pada laki-laki DEU 23:17
-Dan merupakan terjemahan dari bentuk feminin Ibrani dari kata yang diterjemahkan di tempat lain "sodomit" HOS 4:14

subject: STOICISM
entry:
-(A Greek philosophy, inculcating doctrines of severe morality, self-denials, and inconvenience)
-Scripture analogies to
     -John the Baptist wore camel' s hair and subsisted on locusts and wild honey MAT 3:4
     -Came "neither eating nor drinking," MAT 11:18; LUK 7:33
-Jesus requires self-denial and "crosses," MAT 10:38,39; 16:24; MRK 8:34,35; LUK 9:23-26; 14:27
-The subordination of natural affection MAT 10:37; LUK 14:26
-Paul teaches
     -That the "law of the mind" is at war with the "law of the members," ROM 7:23; with 7:14-24
     -That the body must be kept under subjection 1CO 9:27
     -Celibacy 1CO 7:1-9,25,26,32,33,39,40
-School of, at Athens ACT 17:18
-See ASCETICISM
entry_id:
-(Filosofi Yunani, yang menanamkan doktrin moralitas yang keras, penyangkalan diri, dan ketidaknyamanan)

     -Yohanes Pembaptis memakai bulu unta dan hidup dari belalang dan madu hutan MAT 3:4
     -Datang "tidak makan atau minum," MAT 11:18; Lukas 7:33
-Yesus menuntut penyangkalan diri dan "salib", MAT 10:38,39; 16:24; Mrk 8:34,35; Lukas 9:23-26; 14:27
-Penundukan kasih sayang alami MAT 10:37; Lukas 14:26
-Paulus mengajar

     -Bahwa tubuh harus tetap tunduk 1CO 9:27
     -Selibat 1CO 7:1-9,25,26,32,33,39,40
-Sekolah, di Athena ACT 17:18

subject: SUPERSTITION
entry:
-General scriptures concerning ACT 25:19
-INSTANCES OF
     -Israelites, supposing that their defeat in battle with the Philistines was due to their not having brought with them the ark of the covenant 1SA 4:3; with 4:10,11
     -Philistines, refusing to step on the threshold of the temple of Dagon after the image of Dagon had repeatedly fallen upon it 1SA 5:5
     -The belief of the Syrians concerning the help of the gods 1KI 20:23
     -Jews, attributing their calamities to having ceased offering sacrifices to the Queen of Heaven JER 44:17-19
     -Nebuchadnezzar, supposing that the spirit of the gods was upon Daniel DAN 4:8,9
     -The sailors who threw Jonah into the sea JON 1:4-16
     -The disciples, supposing they saw a ghost when Jesus came walking upon the lake MAT 14:26; MRK 6:49,50
     -Herod Antipas, imagining that Jesus was actually John the Baptist risen from the dead MRK 6:14,16
     -The Gadarenes (Gerasenes, Gergasenes), because Jesus expelled demons out of the man who lived among the tombs MAT 8:34
     -The disciples who were frightened at the appearance of Peter ACT 12:14,15
     -The Ephesians, in their sorceries ACT 19:13-19
     -The people of the island of Melita (Malta), in assuming that Paul was some sort of god ACT 28:6
     -See IDOLATRY
     -See SORCERY

entry_id: -Tempat yang lokasinya tidak diketahui 1SA 15:4

subject: TELASSAR
entry:
-Also called THELASAR
-A city or district conquered by the Assyrians 2KI 19:12; ISA 37:12
entry_id:
-Juga disebut THELASAR
-Kota atau distrik yang ditaklukkan oleh bangsa Asyur 2KI 19:12; ISA 37:12

subject: TELEM
entry:
-1. A city of the tribe of Judah JOS 15:24
-2. A porter who put away (divorced) his Gentile wife EZR 10:24
entry_id:
-1. Kota suku Yehuda JOS 15:24
-2. Seorang porter yang menceraikan (menceraikan) istrinya yang bukan Yahudi EZR 10:24

subject: TEL-HARSA
entry:
-Also called TEL-HARESHA
-A place in Babylonia EZR 2:59; NEH 7:61
entry_id:
-Juga disebut TEL-HARESHA
-Sebuah tempat di Babilonia EZR 2:59; NEH 7:61

subject: TEL-MELAH
entry: -A place in Babylonia EZR 2:59; NEH 7:61
entry_id: -Sebuah tempat di Babilonia EZR 2:59; NEH 7:61

subject: TEMA
entry:
-1. Son of Ishmael GEN 25:15; 1CH 1:30
-2. A people of Arabia, probably descendants from Tema, Ishmael' s son JOB 6:19; ISA 21:14; JER 25:23
entry_id:
-1. Son of Ishmael GEN 25:15; 1CH 1:30
-2. Suatu bangsa Arabia, mungkin keturunan Tema, anak Ismael JOB 6:19; ISA 21:14; Yer 25:23

subject: TEMAN
entry:
-1. Son of Eliphaz GEN 36:11
-2. Also called TEMANI and TEMANITES
     -A people supposed to be descended from Teman, son of Eliphaz GEN 36:34; JOB 2:11
     -Prophecies concerning JER 49:7; EZK 25:13; AMO 1:12; OBA 1:9; HAB 3:3
entry_id:
-1. Putra Elifas KEJADIAN 36:11
-2. Disebut juga TEMANI dan TEMANITES
     -Suatu bangsa yang dianggap keturunan Teman, putra Elifas GEN 36:34; PEKERJAAN 2:11
     -Nubuatan tentang JER 49:7; EZK 25:13; AM 1:12; OBA 1:9; HAB 3:3

subject: ZADOK
entry:
-1. The high priest during the time of David' s reign 2SA 19:11; 20:25; 1CH 15:11; 16:39
     -Removes the ark of the covenant from Jerusalem at the time of Absalom' s usurpation; returns with the ark of the covenant at David' s command 2SA 15:24-36; 17:15,17-21
     -Stands aloof from Adonijah at the time of his attempted usurpation 1KI 1:8,26
     -Summoned by David to anoint Solomon to be king 1KI 1:32-40,44,45
     -Performs the function of a high priest after Abiathar was deposed by Solomon 1KI 2:35; 1CH 29:22
-2. Father of Jerusha 2KI 15:33; 2CH 27:1
-3. Son of Ahitub 1CH 6:12
-4. A man of valor 1CH 12:28
-5. Son of Baana NEH 3:4
-6. A priest NEH 3:29

-8. Son of Meraioth NEH 11:11
-9. A treasurer of the temple NEH 13:13

     -Dipanggil oleh Daud untuk mengurapi Sulaiman menjadi raja 1KI 1:32-40,44,45
     -Menjalankan fungsi imam besar setelah Abyatar digulingkan oleh Salomo 1KI 2:35; 1Taw 29:22
-2. Ayah Yerusha 2KI 15:33; 2CH 27:1
-3. Putra Ahitub 1CH 6:12
-4. Seorang yang gagah berani 1CH 12:28

subject: ZEBULUN
entry:
-1. Son of Jacob and Leah GEN 30:20; 35:23; 46:14; 49:13; EXO 1:3; 1CH 2:1
     -Descendants of GEN 46:14; NUM 26:26,27
     -Also called ZABULUN
-2. Tribe of
     -Place of, in marching and camping NUM 2:3,7; 10:14,16
     -Territory awarded to GEN 49:13; JOS 19:10-16; MAT 4:13
     -Aboriginal inhabitants of the territory of, not expelled JDG 1:30
     -Levitical cities of JOS 21:34,35; 1CH 6:77
     -Moses' benediction upon DEU 33:18,19
     -Loyalty of, in resisting the enemies of Israel
     -With Barak against Sisera JDG 4:6,10; 5:14,18
     -With Gideon against the Midianites JDG 6:35
     -With David when made king over Israel 1CH 12:33,38-40
     -Joins with Hezekiah in renewing the Passover 2CH 30:11,18
     -Conquest of, by Tiglath-pileser; carried away to Assyria into captivity 2KI 15:29; ISA 9:1
     -Jesus lived in the land of MAT 4:15
     -Twelve thousand sealed REV 7:8
     -See ISRAEL

     -Juga disebut ZABULUN

     -Dengan Daud ketika dijadikan raja atas Israel 1CH 12:33,38-40
     -Bergabung dengan Hizkia dalam memperbarui Paskah 2CH 30:11,18
     -Penaklukan, oleh Tiglath-pileser; dibawa ke Asyur untuk ditawan 2KI 15:29; ISA 9:1
     -Yesus tinggal di tanah MAT 4:15
     -Dua belas ribu tersegel Wahyu 7:8
     -Lihat ISRAEL

subject: ZEDEKIAH
entry:
-1. Made king of Judah by Nebuchadnezzar 2KI 24:17,18; 1CH 3:15; 2CH 36:10; JER 37:1
     -Throws off his allegiance to Nebuchadnezzar 2KI 24:20; 2CH 36:13; JER 52:3; EZK 17:12-21
     -Forms an alliance with the king of Egypt EZK 17:11-18
     -The allegiance denounced
     -By Jeremiah 2CH 36:12; JER 21; 24:8-10; 27:12-22; 32:3-5; 34; 37:7-10,17; 38:14-28
     -By Ezekiel EZK 12:10-16; 17:12-21
     -Imprisons Jeremiah on account of his denunciations JER 32:2,3; 37:15-21; 38:5-28
     -Seeks the intercession of Jeremiah with God on his behalf JER 21:1-3; 37:3; 38:14-27
     -The evil reign of 2KI 24:19,20; 2CH 36:12,13; JER 37:2; 38:5,19,24-26; 52:2
     -Nebuchadnezzar destroys the city and temple, takes him captive to Babylon, blinds his eyes, kills his sons 2KI 25:1-10; 2CH 36:17-20; JER 1:3; 32:1,2; 39:1-10; 51:59; 52:4-30
-2. Grandson of Jehoiakim 1CH 3:16
-3. A false prophet JER 29:21-23
-4. A prince of Judah JER 36:12
-5. A false prophet
     -Prophesies to Ahab that he will be victorious over the Syrians, instead of being defeated 1KI 22:11; 2CH 18:10
     -Strikes Micaiah, the true prophet 1KI 22:24; 2CH 18:23
entry_id:
-1. Dijadikan raja Yehuda oleh Nebukadnezar 2KI 24:17,18; 1Taw 3:15; 2Taw 36:10; Yer 37:1
     -Melepaskan kesetiaannya kepada Nebukadnezar 2KI 24:20; 2Taw 36:13; Yer 52:3; EZK 17:12-21
     -Membentuk aliansi dengan raja Mesir EZK 17:11-18
     -Kesetiaan dikecam
     -Oleh Yeremia 2CH 36:12; JER 21; 24:8-10; 27:12-22; 32:3-5; 34; 37:7-10,17; 38:14-28
     -Oleh Yehezkiel EZK 12:10-16; 17:12-21
     -Memenjarakan Yeremia karena pengaduannya JER 32:2,3; 37:15-21; 38:5-28
     -Mencari perantaraan Yeremia dengan Tuhan atas namanya YER 21:1-3; 37:3; 38:14-27
     -Pemerintahan jahat 2KI 24:19,20; 2Taw 36:12,13; Yer 37:2; 38:5,19,24-26; 52:2
     -Nebukadnezar menghancurkan kota dan kuil, membawanya sebagai tawanan ke Babel, membutakan matanya, membunuh anak-anaknya 2KI 25:1-10; 2Taw 36:17-20; Yer 1:3; 32:1,2; 39:1-10; 51:59; 52:4-30
-2. Cucu Yoyakim 1CH 3:16
-3. Nabi palsu YER 29:21-23
-4. Seorang pangeran Yehuda JER 36:12
-5. Seorang nabi palsu
     -Bernubuat kepada Ahab bahwa ia akan menang atas orang Aram, bukannya dikalahkan 1KI 22:11; 2Taw 18:10
     -Menyerang Mikha, nabi sejati 1KI 22:24; 2CH 18:23

subject: ZERUBBABEL
entry:
-Also called SHESHBAZZAR
-Directs the rebuilding of the altar and temple after his return from captivity in Babylon EZR 3:2-8; 4:2,3; 5:2,14-16; HAG 1:12-14
-Leads the Emancipated Jews back from Babylon EZR 1:8-11; 2; NEH 12
-Appoints the Levites to inaugarate the rebuilding of the temple EZR 3:2-8
-Prophecies relating to HAG 2:2; ZEC 4:6-10
-Called ZOROBABEL in the genealogy of Joseph MAT 1:12; LUK 3:27
entry_id:
-Juga disebut SHESHBAZZAR
-Mengarahkan pembangunan kembali altar dan kuil setelah dia kembali dari pembuangan di Babel EZR 3:2-8; 4:2,3; 5:2,14-16; HAG 1:12-14
-Memimpin orang-orang Yahudi yang Dibebaskan kembali dari Babilonia EZR 1:8-11; 2; NEH 12
-Menunjuk orang Lewi untuk meresmikan pembangunan kembali Bait Suci EZR 3:2-8
-Nubuatan yang berkaitan dengan HAG 2:2; ZEC 4:6-10
-Disebut ZOROBABEL dalam silsilah Yusuf MAT 1:12; Lukas 3:27

subject: ZION
entry:
-(A stronghold of Jerusalem)
-Also called SION
-Captured from the Jebusites by David 2SA 5:6-9; 1CH 11:5-7
-Called thereafter "the city of David," 2SA 5:7,9; 6:12,16; 1KI 8:1; 1CH 11:5,7; 15:1,29; 2CH 5:2
-The ark of the covenant
     -Placed in 2SA 6:12,16; 1KI 8:1; 1CH 15:1,29; 2CH 5:2
     -Removed from, to Solomon' s temple on Mount Moriah 1KI 8:1; 2CH 5:2; with 3:1
-Collectively, the place, the forms, and the assemblies of Israelite worship 2KI 19:21,31; PSA 9:11; 48:2,11,12; 74:2; 132:13; 137:1; ISA 35:10; 40:9; 49:14; 51:16; 52:1,2,7,8; 60:14; 62:1,11; JER 31:6; 50:5; LAM 1:4; JOL 2:1,15; MAT 21:5; JHN 12:15; ROM 9:33; 11:26; 1PE 2:6
-Name of, applied to Jerusalem PSA 87:2,5; 149:2; So 3:11; ISA 33:14,20; JER 9:19; 30:17; ZEC 9:13
-Called the city of God PSA 87:2,3; ISA 60:14
-Restoration of, promised ISA 51:3,11,16; 52:1,2,7,8; 59:20; 60:14; OBA 1:17-21; ZEP 3:14,16; ZEC 1:14,17; 2:7,10; 8:2,3; 9:9,13
-Name of, applied to the city of the redeemed HEB 12:22; REV 14:1
-See CHURCH
-See JERUSALEM
entry_id:
-(Benteng Yerusalem)
-Juga disebut SION
-Ditangkap dari orang Yebus oleh Daud 2SA 5:6-9; 1Taw 11:5-7
-Selanjutnya disebut "kota Daud", 2SA 5:7,9; 6:12,16; 1KI 8:1; 1Taw 11:5,7; 15:1,29; 2CH 5:2
-Tabut perjanjian
     -Ditempatkan dalam 2SA 6:12,16; 1KI 8:1; 1Taw 15:1,29; 2CH 5:2
     -Dipindahkan dari, ke kuil Sulaiman di Gunung Moria 1KI 8:1; 2CH 5:2; dengan 3:1
-Secara kolektif tempat, bentuk, dan majelis ibadah orang Israel 2KI 19:21,31; PSA 9:11; 48:2,11,12; 74:2; 132:13; 137:1; ISA 35:10; 40:9; 49:14; 51:16; 52:1,2,7,8; 60:14; 62:1,11; Yer 31:6; 50:5; LAM 1:4; Yoh 2:1,15; MAT 21:5; Yohanes 12:15; ROM 9:33; 11:26; 1Pe 2:6
-Nama, diterapkan pada Yerusalem PSA 87:2,5; 149:2; Jadi 3:11; ISA 33:14,20; Yer 9:19; 30:17; ZEC 9:13
-Disebut kota Tuhan PSA 87:2,3; ISA 60:14
-Pemulihan, yang dijanjikan ISA 51:3,11,16; 52:1,2,7,8; 59:20; 60:14; OBA 1:17-21; ZEP 3:14,16; ZEC 1:14,17; 2:7,10; 8:2,3; 9:9,13
-Nama, diterapkan pada kota orang yang ditebus Ibr 12:22; Wahyu 14:1
-Lihat GEREJA
-Lihat YERUSALEM
